size_guide_rows.notes: Best for very small hands; allow a little extra room if using liner gloves.
Snug performance fit; size up if you prefer a more relaxed glove.

Ideal if you’re between M and L and like a bit more room around the fingers.
Largest standard size; check stretch panels if you’re at the upper end of the range.

articles.title: 8-Week 100km Road Cycling Training Plan (Intermediate)
articles.subtitle: Build to your first metric century in just two months
articles.excerpt: This 8-week intermediate plan is designed to take you from regular weekend rides to confidently completing a 100km road event.
articles.content: Over eight weeks you will complete three key sessions per week: endurance, tempo, and skills. Recovery weeks are built in every third week. Use this plan alongside a sensible strength routine and adequate rest.

articles.title: 12-Week 160km Century Training Plan (Advanced)
articles.subtitle: Step up from metric centuries to the full 160km challenge
articles.excerpt: A structured 12-week program for experienced riders targeting a full road century.
articles.content: This plan assumes you can already comfortably ride 80–100km. It layers in back-to-back long rides, threshold intervals, and tapering to arrive fresh on event day.

articles.title: 6-Week Mountain Bike Fundamentals Plan (Beginner)
articles.subtitle: Build skills and fitness for your first singletrack adventures
articles.excerpt: A skills-first MTB plan focused on confidence, braking, cornering, and short climbs.
articles.content: Each week combines one skills session in a safe environment, one short trail ride, and one off-bike strength workout. Expect plenty of low-pressure repetition.

articles.title: 10-Week Indoor Sweet Spot Plan for Busy Riders
articles.subtitle: Maintain strong road fitness with just 3 turbo sessions per week
articles.excerpt: Ideal for winter or busy periods, this plan emphasises time-efficient sweet spot intervals on the trainer.
articles.content: Most sessions are 45–75 minutes with warm-up, sweet spot blocks, and short cool-down. One optional longer weekend indoor ride is included for those with extra time.

articles.title: Disc Brake Maintenance: Quiet, Powerful Stopping in 7 Steps
articles.subtitle: A modern guide to keeping your road and MTB disc brakes running silently
articles.excerpt: Learn how to clean rotors, reset pistons, and bed in new pads so your disc brakes stay consistent and quiet.
articles.content: In this tutorial we cover visual inspection, rotor cleaning, pad alignment, piston reset, and proper bedding-in. You’ll need basic tools, isopropyl alcohol, and a clean workspace.

articles.title: How to Bleed Hydraulic Disc Brakes at Home
articles.subtitle: Step-by-step bleeding guide for common road and MTB systems
articles.excerpt: Spongy levers or inconsistent bite point? A careful bleed can restore firm, predictable braking.
articles.content: We walk through preparing your workspace, fitting the bleed kit, pushing fluid through the system, and disposing of old brake fluid safely.

articles.title: Deep-Clean Your Drivetrain in Under 30 Minutes
articles.subtitle: Make your bike quieter and faster with a proper chain and cassette clean
articles.excerpt: A dirty drivetrain wastes watts and wears out components. Here’s how to fix it without special tools.
articles.content: We cover degreasing the chain, brushing cassette and jockey wheels, rinsing safely, and choosing the right lube for your conditions.

articles.title: How to Fix Saddle Pain: Stop Numbness and Discomfort
articles.subtitle: Practical changes to your position, technique, and equipment
articles.excerpt: Saddle pain isn’t something you just have to live with. Small adjustments to bike fit and posture can make a huge difference.
articles.content: We break down the main causes of saddle discomfort, from saddle shape and tilt to handlebar drop and core stability. Follow the steps and test changes one at a time.

articles.title: Rim Brake Setup Essentials: Centering, Pad Toe-In, and Cable Tension
articles.subtitle: Get reliable braking from classic calipers and cantilevers
articles.excerpt: If your rim brakes feel weak or rub the rim, this guide will help you restore confidence-inspiring stopping power.
articles.content: Learn how to center the brake arms, set appropriate toe-in on pads, and dial in cable tension while avoiding common mistakes.

articles.title: Best Summer Cycling Gloves 2024: Stay Cool and Comfortable
articles.subtitle: We test 10 pairs of lightweight mitts for hot-weather riding
articles.excerpt: From super-breathable mesh backs to cleverly placed palm padding, these are our favourite summer cycling gloves for 2024.
articles.content: In this review we compare fit, ventilation, bar feel, and durability. Use the size guide below to match your palm circumference to the right size before ordering.

articles.title: Best Commuter Helmets 2025: Safer, Cooler City Riding
articles.subtitle: Our top picks for daily rides to work and around town
articles.excerpt: We test commuter helmets for visibility, ventilation, comfort, and value to help you choose the right lid for daily use.
articles.content: This buyer’s guide compares weight, safety features, and commuting-friendly touches like integrated lights and reflective details.

articles.title: Urban Commuter Helmet Buying Guide
articles.subtitle: What to look for in a safe, stylish city helmet
articles.excerpt: MIPS, lights, visors, ventilation—this guide explains which features matter for everyday city riding.
articles.content: We walk through key safety standards, fit tips, and feature trade-offs, plus share our favourite models for different budgets.

articles.title: Winter Cycling Gloves Size Guide
articles.subtitle: Measure once, ride warm all season
articles.excerpt: Thick winter gloves can feel clumsy if the sizing is off. Use this guide to get a snug but warm fit.
articles.content: We explain how to measure your palm circumference in centimetres and match it to unisex winter glove sizing, with tips for layering liners underneath.

articles.title: Best Road Bike Tires 2024: Fast, Grippy, and Puncture-Resistant
articles.subtitle: From wet-weather commuters to race-day slicks
articles.excerpt: We lab-test rolling resistance, wet grip, and puncture protection to find the best road tires for every kind of rider.
articles.content: Use the comparison table filters to narrow down by price, wet-grip rating, and puncture protection. We include both clincher and tubeless-ready options.

articles.title: Best GPS Bike Computers 2025: From Budget to Endurance Monsters
articles.subtitle: Compare mapping, battery life, and training features
articles.excerpt: We test the latest GPS units for battery life, ease of use, and connectivity to help you find the right head unit.
articles.content: Our comparison table highlights battery life, screen size, navigation features, and sensor compatibility so you can quickly shortlist the best options.

articles.title: Best Bike Lights 2025: See and Be Seen
articles.subtitle: Top front and rear USB lights for commuting and training
articles.excerpt: From budget commuters to high-output night-riding setups, these are the best USB bike lights we’ve tested.
articles.content: We split our recommendations into front, rear, and combo setups, and explain how many lumens you really need for different conditions.

comments.content: Started this plan last week and the structure makes a lot of sense. Love the mix of tempo and endurance rides.
Will this be enough if my current long ride is only about 50km? Event is mid-November so timing looks perfect.
@RoadieTom same here. The recovery week in week 4 felt easy on paper but really helped me absorb the work.
Ive tried three different saddles and still get numbness after about an hour. Tilt and height changes helped a bit, but Im still struggling on longer rides.
Great breakdown. I never realised how much bar height affected saddle pressure until I raised mine a couple of spacers.
@Rider42 if you can, book a proper bike fit. My numbness disappeared once my reach and setback were dialled in, even on the same saddle.
Also worth checking your shorts and chamois cream routine. For me, good shorts + gradual time in the saddle was key.
Followed this step-by-step and finally got rid of the embarrassing screeching on my winter bike. Thanks!
Same here. The piston reset trick was the missing step Id never done before.
Swapped to the AquaGrip Road Shield after reading this and the wet cornering confidence is night and day compared to my old tires.
Leaning towards the Enduro Max 400 for the battery life, but wondering if the mapping is overkill for mainly road use.
@DataDriven I got the Enduro Max for long brevets. Mapping is nice to have, but the huge battery is the real win.
Really appreciate the size guide. My palm is exactly 20 cm and the recommended size fits spot on.
Ended up choosing the Metro MIPS after reading this. The fit dial is super comfortable for daily rides.
The photos and order of steps made bleeding my road bike for the first time much less intimidating.
Glad you split out see vs be seen lights. Picked the NightBeam 400 front and CitySafe Blinker rear combo and its perfect for dark city rides.
